title: Fix RawFileSlice idx and tags in lexer_test
body:
While running the lexer test in rust mode, these `RawFileSlice` tags came across with type errors. This fixes the order of the `block_idx` and `tag`.

Side effects: None.

Checklist: tests and docs claimed updated.

step_plan.summary: Fix lexer test failures in rust mode by correcting the positional argument ordering when constructing RawFileSlice objects, ensuring block_idx is an int and tag is Optional[str]. The change is confined to lexer_test test data setup.

steps.operation: Fix RawFileSlice construction to use correct block_idx/tag ordering (prefer keyword args) in the lexer slicing test
steps.reason: RawFileSlice is a NamedTuple with field order (raw, slice_type, source_idx, block_idx=0, tag=None). The test currently passes positional arguments as (..., None, 0), which assigns None to block_idx (should be int) and 0 to tag (should be Optional[str]), causing type errors in rust mode. Switching to keyword args (block_idx=0, tag=None) or swapping to (..., 0, None) restores type correctness while preserving slice coverage invariants (source_idx and raw lengths).
steps.side_effects: Only affects test data construction. May change the string representation of failing assertions if any snapshots/logging include RawFileSlice tuple positions. Ensures the test passes in both python and rust lexer modes; no runtime/library API changes expected.
